context: Tests whether the agent writes well-structured conventional commit messages for a set of described changes, including appropriate use of types, scopes, imperative mood, bodies for complex changes, and subject line length.

checklist.name: Conventional format
checklist.description: All 5 commit messages follow the <type>(<scope>): <description> or <type>: <description> format

checklist.name: Valid types
checklist.description: All 5 commit messages use only approved types from: feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test

checklist.name: Imperative mood
checklist.description: All 5 commit subject lines use imperative mood (e.g. 'add', 'fix') not past tense or gerunds

checklist.name: Subject line length
checklist.description: All 5 subject lines are 72 characters or fewer

checklist.name: Scope on notification service
checklist.description: Commit #1 (notification service) includes a scope in the header (e.g. 'notifications' or 'notification-service')

checklist.name: Body explains polling interval
checklist.description: Commit #1 includes a body that mentions the reason for the 30-second polling interval (load testing or database connection spikes)

checklist.name: Body explains nullable columns
checklist.description: Commit #2 (schema change) includes a body explaining why the columns are nullable (backwards compatibility)

checklist.name: Scope on schema change
checklist.description: Commit #2 (schema) includes a scope reflecting the database or users module
